senses.definition: the act or process of reaching the bottom or becoming the bottom part of something
examples: The bottoming of the pot reveals the sediment at the base.
Bottoming out the curve indicates the car has reached its lowest point.

senses.definition: plural of bottoming; instances or types of the act of reaching the bottom
examples: Several bottomings were observed in the sediment layers.
Different bottomings of the stock market were noted in the report.

senses.definition: low-lying land along a river or floodplain
examples: They farm on the fertile bottomland near the river.
Floods can threaten the bottomland during the rainy season.

senses.definition: plural of bottomland; low-lying river valleys
examples: The bottomlands were rich in nutrients.
Developers mapped the bottomlands before construction.

senses.definition: without a bottom; endlessly deep or large; unable to be satisfied
examples: The bottomless pit terrified the explorers.
She had bottomless curiosity about the universe.
The project seemed to have bottomless funding.

senses.definition: in a bottomless manner; without end or limit
examples: The reservoir fills bottomlessly after heavy rains.
The price continued bottomlessly high.

senses.definition: the state or quality of being bottomless; infinite depth or extent
examples: The bottomlessness of the canyon amazed the hikers.
Her bottomlessness of questions challenged the lecturer.

senses.definition: plural form of bottomlessness; multiple instances of bottomlessness
examples: The essay explored several bottomlessnesses of the universe.
Different cultures spoke of varying bottomlessnesses in myth.

senses.definition: nearest to the bottom; lowest in position
examples: The bottommost shelf held the oldest books.
They stood at the bottommost row of the stadium.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being at the bottom
examples: The bottomness of the trough prevents overflow.
They measured the bottomness of the container.

senses.definition: plural of bottomness; multiple instances of bottomness
examples: Different samples showed varying bottomnesses.
The report notes several bottomnesses in the data.

senses.definition: historical or dialectal form meaning having a bottom; not common in modern usage
examples: The term bottomried appears in old maritime glossaries.
Bottomried lines are not standard in contemporary English.

senses.definition: plural of bottomry; a historical term in marine insurance (see note)
examples: Bottomries were once used to provide loans secured by the bottom of a ship.
The practice of bottomries declined over centuries.

senses.definition: a historical loan in marine insurance secured by the ship or its cargo
examples: The ship owner obtained a bottomry loan to finance repairs.
Bottomry is seldom used in modern law outside historical contexts.

senses.definition: the act of giving or receiving a bottomry loan
examples: Bottomrying was common in medieval maritime commerce.
Records show several instances of bottomrying in port ledgers.

senses.definition: the lower part of something; the part that touches the ground
examples: The fish swam near the bottoms of the lake.
He cleaned the bottoms of the pots.

senses.definition: the set of bottoms in a collection or the bottom row in a display (rare; not common in modern usage)
examples: The bottomset of the display included the smallest items.
In geology, a bottomset layer refers to deposition at the base.

senses.definition: a historical or rare term; likely a hook or fastener related to the bottom of clothing or gear
examples: The old garment used a bottonhook for fastening.
Bottonhook designs appear in antique hardware catalogs.

senses.definition: having a bottony shape or characteristics; related to button-like knobs
examples: The bottony ornaments on the crown caught the eye.
Bottony leaves form a decorative pattern.

senses.definition: plural of bott or a surname; potentially dialect for 'botan seeds' or 'boats' depending on context
examples: The Botts family owned the land.
In dialect, botts may refer to small pebbles.

senses.definition: a stick or rod used at the bottom, possibly a local term or misspelling
examples: The farmer used a bottstick to mark the boundary.
Bottstick appears in regional glossaries.

senses.definition: unclear or highly obscure term; possibly a misspelling or dialect form
examples: Bottu is not widely attested in modern dictionaries.

senses.definition: informal term for buttocks or butt; can be used playfully
examples: He patted his botty and sat down.
The children joked about their botty cushions.

senses.definition: shaped like or resembling a bottle (botulus = sausage or bottle in Latin); botulinum toxin related term is separate
examples: Botuliform structures are observed in some fossils.
The botuliform plant galls resemble little bottles.

senses.definition: a toxin produced by Clostridium botulinum; one of the most potent known toxins
examples: Botulinum toxin is used medically in tiny doses for cosmetic procedures.
Handling botulin requires extreme care in labs.
